channel.title: Theresa W Cheng
channel.description: Recent content on Theresa W Cheng

item.title: Translating between trauma questionnaires
item.description: Note: I hope to make public access to the app available as soon as possible! Currently the link is broken and will be instated pending approvals from those that generally shared the data, as well as the publisher. Two of the most common surveys used to evaluate childhood adversity are the Childhood Trauma Questionaire (CTQ) and Adverse Childhood Experience (ACE) questionnaires. For each of five maltreatment subscales, the CTQ has multiple items corresponding to a single ACE item.

item.title: Visualizing the translation between trauma questionnaires
item.description: These four visualizations helped me make sense of the data. Not all of them made it to the final Shiny App. Not sure what I’m talking about? Check out my post here [bananas link!] 1. Describing the Childhood Trauma Questionnaire (CTQ) subscales using heatmaps Before going about trying to convert between the CTQ and ACE survey, I wanted to make sure that items on the CTQ “hung together” within subscales.

item.title: Hello R Markdown
item.description: R Markdown This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com. You can embed an R code chunk like this: summary(cars) ## speed dist ## Min. : 4.0 Min. : 2.00 ## 1st Qu.:12.0 1st Qu.: 26.00 ## Median :15.0 Median : 36.00 ## Mean :15.4 Mean : 42.98 ## 3rd Qu.
